devanagari: राजसूयोपक्रमे पाण्डवानां दिग्विजयः भीमेन जरासंध वधश्च । श्रीशुक उवाच । एकदा तु सभामध्य आस्थितो मुनिभिर्वृतः । ब्राह्मणैः क्षत्रियैर्वैश...
english_devnagari: rājasūyopakrame pāṇḍavānāṃ digvijayaḥ bhīmena jarāsaṃdha vadhaśca śrīśuka uvāca ekadā tu sabhāmadhya āsthito munibhirvṛtaḥ brāhmaṇaiḥ kṣatriyairvaiśyaiḥ bhrātṛbhiśca yudhiṣṭhiraḥ

devanagari: आचार्यैः कुलवृद्धैश्च ज्ञातिसंबन्धिबान्धवैः । श्रृण्वतामेव चैतेषां आभाष्येदमुवाच ह ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: ācāryaiḥ kulavṛddhaiśca jñātisaṃbandhibāndhavaiḥ śrṛṇvatāmeva caiteṣāṃ ābhāṣyedamuvāca ha

devanagari: श्रीयुधिष्ठिर उवाच । क्रतुराजेन गोविन्द राजसूयेन पावनीः । यक्ष्ये विभूतीर्भवतः तत्संपादय नः प्रभो ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīyudhiṣṭhira uvāca kraturājena govinda rājasūyena pāvanīḥ yakṣye vibhūtīrbhavataḥ tatsaṃpādaya naḥ prabho

translation:
Yudhiṣṭhira said:
O Lord Govinda! I intend to adore you and your hallowed part-manifestations (in the form of Indra and other deities) by performing the noblest of sacrifice called the Rāja- sūya. May you be pleased (to help us) to accomplish this.

devanagari: त्वत्पादुके अविरतं परि ये चरन्ति । ध्यायन्त्यभद्रनशने शुचयो गृणन्ति । विन्दन्ति ते कमलनाभ भवापवर्गम् । आशासते यदि त आशिष ईश नान्ये ॥ ४ ...
english_devnagari: tvatpāduke avirataṃ pari ye caranti dhyāyantyabhadranaśane śucayo gṛṇanti vindanti te kamalanābha bhavāpavargam āśāsate yadi ta āśiṣa īśa nānye

[nominative plural from √ vid class 7 verb], [vocative plural from √ vid class 7 verb], [accusative plural from √ vid class 7 verb]

translation: O lotus-navelled Lord? Your feet destroy everything inauspicious. Those pious people who constantly worship them physically, contemplate on them mentally, and sing of them vocally attain emancipation from saṃsāra. They obtain even worldly blessings if they so desire but not others.

devanagari: तद्देवदेव भवतश्चरणारविन्द । सेवानुभावमिह पश्यतु लोक एषः । ये त्वां भजन्ति न भजन्त्युत वोभयेषां । निष्ठां प्रदर्शय विभो कुरुसृञ्जयानाम् ...
english_devnagari: taddevadeva bhavataścaraṇāravinda sevānubhāvamiha paśyatu loka eṣaḥ ye tvāṃ bhajanti na bhajantyuta vobhayeṣāṃ niṣṭhāṃ pradarśaya vibho kurusṛñjayānām
translation: O God of gods! Let the world, therefore, witness the supreme efficacy of the service of your lotus-like feet. O Lord! Be pleased to demonstrate to the Kaurava and Sṛñjaya chiefs, the status reached and fruits reaped by those who worship you and by those who do not do so.

devanagari: न ब्रह्मणः स्वपरभेदमतिस्तव स्यात् । सर्वात्मनः समदृशः स्वसुखानुभूतेः । संसेवतां सुरतरोरिव ते प्रसादः । सेवानुरूपमुदयो न विपर्ययोऽत्र ॥ ...
english_devnagari: na brahmaṇaḥ svaparabhedamatistava syāt sarvātmanaḥ samadṛśaḥ svasukhānubhūteḥ saṃsevatāṃ surataroriva te prasādaḥ sevānurūpamudayo na viparyayo
translation: O Lord! You are Brahman himself, the Inner Soul abiding in all beings. You eternally enjoy your essential state of blissfulness and look upon all as equal. Hence the notion of difference such as ‘this is mine’ and ‘this is not mine’ cannot be predicated of you. Like persons betaking to the celestial wish-yielding tree, your grace is conferred on those who serve you and it is commensurate to the measure (and intensity) of their service, and there is no violation of this law.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । सम्यग् व्यवसितं राजन् भवता शत्रुकर्शन । कल्याणी येन ते कीर्तिः लोकान् अनुभविष्यति ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca samyag vyavasitaṃ rājan bhavatā śatrukarśana kalyāṇī yena te kīrtiḥ lokān anubhaviṣyati

translation:
The Lord replied:
O King! This resolution of yours is excellent. O destroyer of enemies? By performance of the Ṛājasūya sacrifice your auspicious fame (as the conqueror of all kings) will pervade the three worlds.

devanagari: ऋषीणां पितृदेवानां सुहृदामपि नः प्रभो । सर्वेषामपि भूतानां ईप्सितः क्रतुराडयम् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: ṛṣīṇāṃ pitṛdevānāṃ suhṛdāmapi naḥ prabho sarveṣāmapi bhūtānāṃ īpsitaḥ kraturāḍayam
translation: The performance of this sovereign sacrifice is desirable not only on behalf of us, your friends and well wishers, but also on behalf of sages, your ancestors, gods and all created beings.

devanagari: विजित्य नृपतीन् सर्वान् कृत्वा च जगतीं वशे । सम्भृत्य सर्वसम्भारान् आहरस्व महाक्रतुम् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: vijitya nṛpatīn sarvān kṛtvā ca jagatīṃ vaśe sambhṛtya sarvasambhārān āharasva mahākratum
translation: Having defeated all the kings in the world and thus brought under control the whole of the earth, collect all the necessary articles of sacrifice and then commence the performance of this great sacrifice.

devanagari: एते ते भ्रातरो राजन् लोकपालांशसंभवाः । जितोऽस्म्यात्मवता तेऽहं दुर्जयो योऽकृतात्मभिः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: ete te bhrātaro rājan lokapālāṃśasaṃbhavāḥ jito

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ ji class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ ji class 9 verb]

translation: O King! These brothers of yours are born from the rays of the deities (such as Indra, Vāyu) who are the guardians of the worlds. And I, who am never won over by persons lacking self-control, have been conquered by you, by your control of mind and senses.

devanagari: न कश्चिन्मत्परं लोके तेजसा यशसा श्रिया । विभूतिभिर्वाभिभवेद् देवोऽपि किमु पार्थिवः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: na kaścinmatparaṃ loke tejasā yaśasā śriyā vibhūtibhirvābhibhaved devo
translation: In this world, not even a celestial being can discomfit my devotee by his energy, glory, affluence or supernatural power, much less can an earthly being do so.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । निशम्य भगवद्गीतं प्रीतः फुल्लमुखाम्बुजः । भ्रातॄन् दिग्विजयेऽयुङ्क्त विष्णुतेजोपबृंहितान् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca niśamya bhagavadgītaṃ prītaḥ phullamukhāmbujaḥ bhrātṝn digvijaye

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Having heard the approbatory speech of the Lord, king Yudhiṣṭhira became extremely delighted and his countenance looked cheerful like a fresh-blown lotus. He assigned the conquest of cardinal points (of the world) to his brothers who were imbued with the lustre and energy of god Viṣṇu.

devanagari: सहदेवं दक्षिणस्यां आदिशत् सह सृञ्जयैः । दिशि प्रतीच्यां नकुलं उदीच्यां सव्यसाचिनम् । प्राच्यां वृकोदरं मत्स्यैः केकयैः सह मद्रकैः ॥ १३...
english_devnagari: sahadevaṃ dakṣiṇasyāṃ ādiśat saha sṛñjayaiḥ diśi pratīcyāṃ nakulaṃ udīcyāṃ savyasācinam prācyāṃ vṛkodaraṃ matsyaiḥ kekayaiḥ saha madrakaiḥ
translation: He commanded Sahadeva to conquer the South along with Sṛñjayas and deputed Nakula to the West, and Arjuna to the North and Bhīma, in alliance with Matsyas, Kekayas and Madrakas to the East.

devanagari: ते विजित्य नृपान् वीरा आजह्रुर्दिग्भ्य ओजसा । अजातशत्रवे भूरि द्रविणं नृप यक्ष्यते ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: te vijitya nṛpān vīrā ājahrurdigbhya ojasā ajātaśatrave bhūri draviṇaṃ nṛpa yakṣyate
translation: Conquering the kings with their might and energy, those warriors brought from those quarters immense wealth to king Yudhiṣṭhira who was preparing for sacrifice.

devanagari: श्रुत्वाजितं जरासन्धं नृपतेर्ध्यायतो हरिः । आहोपायं तमेवाद्य उद्धवो यमुवाच ह ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: śrutvājitaṃ jarāsandhaṃ nṛpaterdhyāyato hariḥ āhopāyaṃ tamevādya uddhavo yamuvāca ha

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [locative single from √ vad class 1 verb], [locative single from √ vad ]

translation: Hearing that Jarāsandha could not be conquered, Yudhiṣṭhira became anxious. They say that to Yudhiṣṭhira who was pondering (over the subjugation of Jarāsandha), Kṛṣṇa, the Primary Cause of the world, suggested the expedient suggested by Uddhava.

devanagari: भीमसेनोऽर्जुनः कृष्णो ब्रह्मलिङ्गधरास्त्रयः । जग्मुर्गिरिव्रजं तात बृहद्रथसुतो यतः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: bhīmaseno jagmurgirivrajaṃ tāta bṛhadrathasuto yataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: O Parīkṣit? Thereupon, the three (warriors)—Bhīmasena, Arjuna and Kṛṣṇa—disguised themselves as Brāhmaṇas and went to Girivraja (the capital of Magadha) where stayed Jarāsandha, the son of Bṛhadratha.

devanagari: ते गत्वाऽतिथ्यवेलायां गृहेषु गृहमेधिनम् । ब्रह्मण्यं समयाचेरन् राजन्या ब्रह्मलिङ्गिनः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: te gatvā brahmaṇyaṃ samayāceran rājanyā brahmaliṅginaḥ
translation: Those three Kṣattriyas disguised as Brāhmaṇas, went to Jarāsandha’s palace at the time of receiving unexpected guests (with no previous appointment). They begged of king Jarāsandha who was friendly and devoted to Brāhmaṇas and was a keen observer of the sacred duties of the householder.

devanagari: राजन् विद्ध्यतिथीन् प्राप्तान् अर्थिनो दूरमागतान् । तन्नः प्रयच्छ भद्रं ते यद् वयं कामयामहे ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: rājan viddhyatithīn prāptān arthino dūramāgatān tannaḥ prayaccha bhadraṃ te yad vayaṃ kāmayāmahe

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ pre class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ pre class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ pre class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: O king! Please know us as unexpected guests who have come over a long distance for soliciting a request. May god betide you! Be, therefore, pleased to grant our request.

devanagari: किं दुर्मर्षं तितिक्षूणां किं अकार्यं असाधुभिः । किं न देयं वदान्यानां कः परः समदर्शिनाम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: kiṃ durmarṣaṃ titikṣūṇāṃ kiṃ akāryaṃ asādhubhiḥ kiṃ na deyaṃ vadānyānāṃ kaḥ paraḥ samadarśinām
translation: Is there anything unbearable to people with fortitude and endurance? What act is there which the unrighteous would hesitate to do? What is there that the generous cannot give away? What person is a stranger or an enemy to those who look upon all as equals.

devanagari: योऽनित्येन शरीरेण सतां गेयं यशो ध्रुवम् । नाचिनोति स्वयं कल्पः स वाच्यः शोच्य एव सः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: yo nācinoti svayaṃ kalpaḥ sa vācyaḥ śocya eva saḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ śuc class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ śuc class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ śuc ]

translation: Whoever, endowed with capability to do so, does not, by means of this perishable body, achieve everlasting glory worthy of being sung by the righteous, is worthy of being pitied and censured.

devanagari: हरिश्चन्द्रो रन्तिदेव उञ्छवृत्तिः शिबिर्बलिः । व्याधः कपोतो बहवो ह्यध्रुवेण ध्रुवं गताः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: hariścandro rantideva uñchavṛttiḥ śibirbaliḥ vyādhaḥ kapoto bahavo hyadhruveṇa dhruvaṃ gatāḥ
translation: Kings Hariścandra, Rantideva, Śibi, Bali, Mudgala (who lived on grains gleaned from fields), the legendary pigeon (who sacrificed its life for the hunter and the hunter (who emulated the pigeon’s example) and many others attained to everlasting fame at the cost of this transient body.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । स्वरैराकृतिभिस्तांस्तु प्रकोष्ठैर्ज्याहतैरपि । राजन्यबन्धून् विज्ञाय दृष्टपूर्वानचिन्तयत् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca svarairākṛtibhistāṃstu prakoṣṭhairjyāhatairapi rājanyabandhūn vijñāya dṛṣṭapūrvānacintayat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
From their tones, appearance, the scars on the forearms caused by the friction of bow-strings, Jarāsandha came to know that they belonged to the Kṣattriya class. He also felt that he had seen them formerly somewhere. He began to think:

devanagari: राजन्यबन्धवो ह्येते ब्रह्मलिङ्गानि बिभ्रति । ददानि भिक्षितं तेभ्य आत्मानमपि दुस्त्यजम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: rājanyabandhavo hyete brahmaliṅgāni bibhrati dadāni bhikṣitaṃ tebhya ātmānamapi dustyajam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ bhaj ], [accusative single from √ bhaj ]

translation: “These are certainly Kṣattriyas who have adopted the guise of Brāhmaṇas. I shall give whatever they beg of me even if it be my body which is difficult to part with.

devanagari: बलेर्नु श्रूयते कीर्तिः वितता दिक्ष्वकल्मषा । ऐश्वर्याद् भ्रंशितस्यापि विप्रव्याजेन विष्णुना ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: balernu śrūyate kīrtiḥ vitatā dikṣvakalmaṣā aiśvaryād bhraṃśitasyāpi vipravyājena viṣṇunā
translation: The glorious fame of king Bali is heard glorified far and wide in all directions even though he was dislodged from his sovereign position by Viṣṇu in the disguise of a Brāh- maṇa.

devanagari: श्रियं जिहीर्षतेन्द्रस्य विष्णवे द्विजरूपिणे । जानन्नपि महीं प्रादाद् वार्यमाणोऽपि दैत्यराट् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: śriyaṃ jihīrṣatendrasya viṣṇave dvijarūpiṇe jānannapi mahīṃ prādād vāryamāṇo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [instrumental single from √ hṛ ], [dative single from √ hṛ ]
[instrumental single from √ hṛ ], [dative single from √ hṛ ]
[nominative single from √ jñā class 9 verb], [vocative single from √ jñā class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ prā class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ prā class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ prā class 2 verb]

translation: The great king of Daityas, knowing full well that it was Viṣṇu in the form of a Brāhmaṇa and though prevented by his preceptor Śukra, he gave away the earth to Viṣṇu in the guise of a Brāhmaṇa who intended to deprive Bali of his sovereignty and splendour for the sake of Indra.

devanagari: जीवता ब्राह्मणार्थाय को न्वर्थः क्षत्रबन्धुना । देहेन पतमानेन नेहता विपुलं यशः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: jīvatā brāhmaṇārthāya ko nvarthaḥ kṣatrabandhunā dehena patamānena nehatā vipulaṃ yaśaḥ
translation: What is the good of this life of that wretched Kṣat- triya who does hot aspire to attain vast renown at the cost of this perishable, ever-waning body for the good of Brāhmaṇas.”

devanagari: इत्युदारमतिः प्राह कृष्णार्जुनवृकोदरान् । हे विप्रा व्रियतां कामो ददाम्यात्मशिरोऽपि वः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: ityudāramatiḥ prāha kṛṣṇārjunavṛkodarān he viprā vriyatāṃ kāmo dadāmyātmaśiro
translation: Reflecting thus, the noble-minded king Jarāsandha spoke out to Kṛṣṇa, Arjuna and Bhīma, “O Brāhmaṇas. Choose whatever you desire to have. I shall give even my head to you.”

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । युद्धं नो देहि राजेन्द्र द्वन्द्वशो यदि मन्यसे । युद्धार्थिनो वयं प्राप्ता राजन्या नान्यकाङ्क्षिणः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca yuddhaṃ no dehi rājendra dvandvaśo yadi manyase yuddhārthino vayaṃ prāptā rājanyā nānyakāṅkṣiṇaḥ

translation:
Lord Kṛṣṇa replied:
“O great king. We are Kṣattriyas who have come here to seek a fight. We do not beg for food. We want a fight with you, in a single combat if it pleases you.

devanagari: असौ वृकोदरः पार्थः तस्य भ्रातार्जुनो ह्ययम् । अनयोर्मातुलेयं मां कृष्णं जानीहि ते रिपुम् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: asau vṛkodaraḥ pārthaḥ tasya bhrātārjuno hyayam anayormātuleyaṃ māṃ kṛṣṇaṃ jānīhi te ripum
translation: This is Vṛkodara (Bhīma) the son of Kuntī. This is his brother Arjuna. Please know me as Kṛṣṇa, their maternal cousin and your sworn enemy.”

devanagari: एवमावेदितो राजा जहासोच्चैः स्म मागधः । आह चामर्षितो मन्दा युद्धं तर्हि ददामि वः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: evamāvedito rājā jahāsoccaiḥ sma māgadhaḥ āha cāmarṣito mandā yuddhaṃ tarhi dadāmi vaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Thus informed by Kṛṣṇa, the king of Magadhas laughed loudly. Getting enraged, he declared, “you dull-witted fellows, (if you so seek it) I shall give a battle with you.

devanagari: न त्वया भीरुणा योत्स्ये युधि विक्लवतेजसा । मथुरां स्वपुरीं त्यक्त्वा समुद्रं शरणं गतः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: na tvayā bhīruṇā yotsye yudhi viklavatejasā mathurāṃ svapurīṃ tyaktvā samudraṃ śaraṇaṃ gataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: I shall not fight with a coward like you who get confounded in battles. Out of my fear, you abandoned your capital Mathurā and sought shelter in the sea.

devanagari: अयं तु वयसा तुल्यो नातिसत्त्वो न मे समः । अर्जुनो न भवेद् योद्धा भीमस्तुल्यबलो मम ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: ayaṃ tu vayasā tulyo nātisattvo na me samaḥ arjuno na bhaved yoddhā bhīmastulyabalo mama
translation: This Arjuna is neither equal in age nor surpassing me in strength. And hence he is no match for me as a combatant. Bhīma is my equal in strength.”

devanagari: इत्युक्त्वा भीमसेनाय प्रादाय महतीं गदाम् । द्वितीयां स्वयमादाय निर्जगाम पुराद् बहिः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: ityuktvā bhīmasenāya prādāya mahatīṃ gadām dvitīyāṃ svayamādāya nirjagāma purād bahiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ prā class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ prā class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ prā class 2 verb]

translation: With these words, he handed over a huge mace to Bhīmasena and taking another tremendous mace for himself he went out of the town.

devanagari: ततः समे खले वीरौ संयुक्तावितरेतरौ । जघ्नतुर्वज्रकल्पाभ्यां गदाभ्यां रणदुर्मदौ ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ same khale vīrau saṃyuktāvitaretarau jaghnaturvajrakalpābhyāṃ gadābhyāṃ raṇadurmadau
translation: There on a level ground as the arena, the two warriors closed in fight against each other, both of them, being proud and furious in battle, hit each other with their maces hard like (Indra’s) thunderbolt.

devanagari: मण्डलानि विचित्राणि सव्यं दक्षिणमेव च । चरतोः शुशुभे युद्धं नटयोरिव रङ्गिणोः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: maṇḍalāni vicitrāṇi savyaṃ dakṣiṇameva ca caratoḥ śuśubhe yuddhaṃ naṭayoriva raṅgiṇoḥ

translation: While they were making skilful wheeling manoeuvres to the right or to the left, the combat became spectacular like the one of actors on the stage.

devanagari: ततश्चटचटाशब्दो वज्रनिष्पेषसन्निभः । गदयोः क्षिप्तयो राजन् दन्तयोरिव दन्तिनोः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: tataścaṭacaṭāśabdo vajraniṣpeṣasannibhaḥ gadayoḥ kṣiptayo rājan dantayoriva dantinoḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: O king! When the maces hurled at each other clashed each other, there arose a harsh and cracking sound caṭ caṭ resembling the clapping of Vajras (Indra’s thunderbolt) or like the clashing of the tusks of two fighting tuskers.

devanagari: ते वै गदे भुजजवेन निपात्यमाने । अन्योन्यतोंऽसकटिपादकरोरुजत्रून् । चूर्णीबभूवतुरुपेत्य यथार्कशाखे । संयुध्यतोर्द्विरदयोरिव दीप्तमन्व्यो...
english_devnagari: te vai gade bhujajavena nipātyamāne anyonyatoṃ cūrṇībabhūvaturupetya yathārkaśākhe saṃyudhyatordviradayoriva dīptamanvyoḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Just as two twigs of the sun plant are reduced to pulp when two elephants, furious with rage, fight with each other with those twigs, those two maces hurled with full force of their arms dashed against each other and came in contact with each other’s shoulders, hips, feet, hands, thighs and collar-bones and were reduced to powder.

devanagari: इत्थं तयोः प्रहतयोर्गदयोर्नृवीरौ । क्रुद्धौ स्वमुष्टिभिरयःस्परशैरपिष्टाम् । शब्दस्तयोः प्रहरतोरिभयोरिवासीन् । निर्घातवज्रपरुषस्तलताडनो...
english_devnagari: itthaṃ tayoḥ prahatayorgadayornṛvīrau kruddhau svamuṣṭibhirayaḥsparaśairapiṣṭām śabdastayoḥ praharatoribhayorivāsīn nirghātavajraparuṣastalatāḍanotthaḥ

[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: When their maces were broken down in this way, those furious warriors among men pounded each other, boxing with fists as hard as iron. The noise produced by the blows of their palms as they struck each other was like that of two tuskers dashing against each other and was harsh like the striking of Vajra (or clapping of thunder).

devanagari: तयोरेवं प्रहरतोः समशिक्षाबलौजसोः । निर्विशेषमभूद् युद्धं अक्षीणजवयोर्नृप ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: tayorevaṃ praharatoḥ samaśikṣābalaujasoḥ nirviśeṣamabhūd yuddhaṃ akṣīṇajavayornṛpa

[nominative single from √ yudh class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ yudh class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ yudh class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ yudh class 4 verb]

translation: Both Bhīma and Jarāsandha were equal to each other in training, strength and energy. They struck each other with unabated force. The combat however remained undecided.

devanagari: एवं तयोर्महाराज युध्यतोः सप्तविंशतिः । दिनानि निरगंस्तत्र सुहृद्वन् निशि तिष्ठतोः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ tayormahārāja yudhyatoḥ saptaviṃśatiḥ dināni niragaṃstatra suhṛdvan niśi tiṣṭhatoḥ

translation: In this way twentyseven days passed while they fought by day but behaved like friends at night (when the combat was over).

devanagari: एकदा मातुलेयं वै प्राह राजन् वृकोदरः । न शक्तोऽहं जरासन्धं निर्जेतुं युधि माधव ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: ekadā mātuleyaṃ vai prāha rājan vṛkodaraḥ na śakto

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: O king Parīkṣit! On one day Bhīmasena confessed to Kṛṣṇa, his maternal uncle’s son, “O Mādhava (Descendant of Madhu), I am unable to vanquish Jarāsandha in the duel.

devanagari: शत्रोर्जन्ममृती विद्वान् जीवितं च जराकृतम् । पार्थमाप्याययन् स्वेन तेजसाचिन्तयद्धरिः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: śatrorjanmamṛtī vidvān jīvitaṃ ca jarākṛtam pārthamāpyāyayan svena tejasācintayaddhariḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ jīv class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ jīv class 1 verb]
[imperfect active second dual], [aorist active second dual]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Śrī Kṛṣṇa knew the secret of the birth and death of the enemy and how the demoness Jarā conferred life on Jarāsandha. He strengthened Bhīma by transferring his energy and began to consider.

devanagari: सञ्चिन्त्यारिवधोपायं भीमस्यामोघदर्शनः । दर्शयामास विटपं पाटयन्निव संज्ञया ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: sañcintyārivadhopāyaṃ bhīmasyāmoghadarśanaḥ darśayāmāsa viṭapaṃ pāṭayanniva saṃjñayā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ paṭ ], [vocative single from √ paṭ ]

translation: He hit upon a plan of killing the enemy. Kṛṣṇa of infallible insight showed it to Bhīma symbolically by splitting a twig.

devanagari: तद्विज्ञाय महासत्त्वो भीमः प्रहरतां वरः । गृहीत्वा पादयोः शत्रुं पातयामास भूतले ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: tadvijñāya mahāsattvo bhīmaḥ praharatāṃ varaḥ gṛhītvā pādayoḥ śatruṃ pātayāmāsa bhūtale
translation: Bhīma, a prominent warrior of enormous strength understood the suggestion conveyed to him. He took hold of Jarāsandha by both his feet and felled him on the ground.

devanagari: एकं पादं पदाक्रम्य दोर्भ्यामन्यं प्रगृह्य सः । गुदतः पाटयामास शाखमिव महागजः ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: ekaṃ pādaṃ padākramya dorbhyāmanyaṃ pragṛhya saḥ gudataḥ pāṭayāmāsa śākhamiva mahāgajaḥ
translation: He pressed down one foot of the enemy with one of his feet and with his both hands he caught hold of the other foot and tore him in two from the anus, like an elephant splitting a branch of a tree.

devanagari: एकपादोरुवृषण कटिपृष्ठस्तनांसके । एकबाह्वक्षिभ्रूकर्णे शकले ददृशुः प्रजाः ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: ekapādoruvṛṣaṇa kaṭipṛṣṭhastanāṃsake ekabāhvakṣibhrūkarṇe śakale dadṛśuḥ prajāḥ
translation: Now people beheld the two separate parts of the body each with one foot, one thigh, one testicle, one hip, half the back, one breast, one shoulder, one arm, one eye, one eyebrow and one ear.

devanagari: हाहाकारो महानासीत् निहते मगधेश्वरे । पूजयामासतुर्भीमं परिरभ्य जयाच्यतौ ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: hāhākāro mahānāsīt nihate magadheśvare pūjayāmāsaturbhīmaṃ parirabhya jayācyatau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]

translation: There was a great outcry, when the king of Magadhas was slain. Clasping Bhīma in their arms, Arjuna and Kṛṣṇa applauded him.

devanagari: सहदेवं तत्तनयं भगवान्भूतभावनः । अभ्यषिञ्चदमेयात्मा मगधानां पतिं प्रभुः । मोचयामास राजन्यान् संरुद्धा मागधेन ये ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: sahadevaṃ tattanayaṃ bhagavānbhūtabhāvanaḥ abhyaṣiñcadameyātmā magadhānāṃ patiṃ prabhuḥ mocayāmāsa rājanyān saṃruddhā māgadhena ye
translation: The glorious Lord Kṛṣṇa is the creator and protector of all beings. None could fathom his greatness and power. He installed Jarāsandha’s son Sahadeva as the king of Magadhas on the throne and set at liberty kings kept in confinement by Jarāsandha, the king of Magadhas.

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे उत्तरार्धे । जरासन्ध वधो नाम द्विसप्ततितमोऽध्यायः ॥ ७२ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe uttarārdhe jarāsandha vadho nāma dvisaptatitamo
